comments.text: In [Er75f,p.107], Erdős writes: "Denote by $F(n)$ the smallest integer for which one can color the points of $n$-dimensional space by $F(n)$ colors so that two points of the same color never have distance $1$. NELSON conjectured $F(2) = 4$. W. and L. MOSER proved $F(2)\ge 4$ and it is known that $F(2)\le 6$." So, almost certainly this last equation $F(2)\le 6$ is an error and should have said $\le 7$. ... But what if it wasn't an error?
